Tests that dividing two empty objects results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks if the result of dividing two empty objects is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression `isNaN({} / {})` evaluates to `true`. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the division of non-numeric objects, which should result in `NaN`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific behavior of the JavaScript language related to type coercion and arithmetic operations.
